---
title: "Reports"
subtitle: "Weekly Weather & Soil Water Analysis"
---

## Current Week's Report

The most recent comprehensive report on Arkansas weather conditions and soil water status is updated every Monday morning.

[**📄 View This Week's Report**](current-report.qmd)

---

## Report Contents

Each weekly report includes:

### 1. **Statewide Executive Summary**
- Temperature trends (high, low, mean)
- Precipitation totals and anomalies
- Overall soil water status map
- Key events and alerts

### 2. **Historical Benchmarking**
- This week's percentile ranking vs. 20+ year normal
- How unusual are this week's conditions?
- Record highs/lows and wettest/driest periods
- Seasonal context and trends

### 3. **Station Deep-Dives** (10+ research stations)
For each major station:
- Current conditions (temperature, precipitation, soil water)
- 7-day historical comparison
- 7-10 day forecast
- Growing Degree Days (GDD) accumulation
- Crop stress alerts

### 4. **Forecast Outlook**
- 7-10 day temperature and precipitation trends
- Frost risk dates and probabilities
- Projected water stress periods
- Actionable crop management alerts

### 5. **Data & Visualizations**
- Interactive maps showing statewide conditions
- Time series plots for each station
- Soil water status visualization
- Downloadable CSV datasets

---

## Archive

Browse [past reports](archive.qmd) to see seasonal patterns and year-to-year comparisons.

---

## Weekly Topics

### Spring (March–May)
- Frost risk monitoring
- Soil warming trends
- Planting date optimization
- Early-season water availability

### Summer (June–August)
- Heat stress monitoring
- Irrigation demand forecasting
- Soil moisture depletion tracking
- Growth stage development

### Fall (September–November)
- Maturity acceleration due to cool nights
- Harvest weather outlook
- Grain fill conditions
- End-of-season water status

### Winter (December–February)
- Season-to-date cumulative analysis
- Winter precipitation impacts
- Setup for spring planting

---

## How to Read the Reports

### Understanding Percentile Rankings
- **Below 25th percentile:** Unusually dry/cool (rare for this time of year)
- **25th–75th percentile:** Near-normal conditions (most years fall here)
- **Above 75th percentile:** Unusually wet/warm (rare for this time of year)

### Soil Water Interpretation
- **>75% available water:** Good conditions; low stress risk
- **50–75%:** Adequate; monitor if hot/dry forecast
- **25–50%:** Caution; stress possible if hot/dry continues
- **<25%:** Critical; irrigation recommended if available

### Growing Degree Days (GDD)
Accumulated heat from April 1. Linked to crop growth stages:
- **100 GDD:** Emergence (most crops)
- **400–600 GDD:** V4–V6 leaf growth
- **900–1200 GDD:** Flowering (varies by crop/variety)
- **1500–2000 GDD:** Physiological maturity

---

## Subscribing to Reports

### Email Delivery
Sign up for email delivery of new reports (coming soon):
[Subscribe](mailto:eelli@uark.edu?subject=Subscribe%20to%20Weekly%20Reports)

### RSS Feed
Subscribe to new report notifications:
[RSS Feed](../feed.xml)

---

## Feedback

Have questions about the reports or suggestions for improvements?

[📧 Send Feedback](mailto:eelli@uark.edu?subject=Weekly%20Weather%20Report%20Feedback)
